items.title: Alaska Airlines Narrowly Beats Estimates as Fuel Costs Stay High
items.description: Alaska Air Group Inc. earnings outlook slightly beat analyst predictions as the airline grapples with rising fuel prices from the war in the Middle East.

items.title: Capital One Profit Beats Estimates as Loan-Loss Provisions Drop
items.description: Capital One Financial Corp., the biggest US credit-card lender, swung to a profit in the second quarter as it set aside less money to cover bad loans than Wall Street had anticipated.

items.title: Canadian Stocks Climb as Gold Surge Offsets Trump Tariff Threat
items.description: Canadian stocks climbed Tuesday despite US President Donald Trump threatening 50% tariffs on the country’s exports, thanks to surging gold prices and a rally in tech hardware stocks.

items.title: Subprime Auto Dealer Goes From Covid-Era Rockstar to Near Demise
items.description: America’s Car-Mart Inc. thrived during a ’90s slump, the 2008 financial crisis and the upheaval of Covid-19 by doing one thing well: selling and financing older, high-mileage used cars for cash-strapp

items.title: Treasury Flags Concern Over ‘Potentially Abusive’ Tax Trades
items.description: The US Treasury Department has expressed concern over a number of high-profile tax strategies touted by Wall Street that it says may be “too good to be true.”

items.title: Iberdrola Agrees to Buy 80% Stake in Caruna for €2 Billion
items.description: Iberdrola SA agreed to buy an 80% stake in Finland’s main electricity distribution network Caruna Group for €2 billion ($2.3 billion).

items.title: Oaktree’s Poli Sees a $200 Billion ‘Distressed Moment’ Coming as Rates Stay High
items.description: Companies with $200 billion in debt face a reckoning as funding costs stay high and debt comes due over the next few years, according to distressed specialist Oaktree Capital Management.

items.title: Liverpool FC Owners Consider Selling Stake to Bhatia-Led Group
items.description: The owners of Liverpool FC are considering selling a stake in the English Premier League football club to a consortium of investors led by Amit Bhatia.

items.title: Viktor Shvets on Whether Geopolitics Matters for Investors
items.description: Tail risks are growing, but they aren’t everything.

items.title: Adobe, Salesforce Downgrades Are Latest Show of AI Fears
items.description: Concerns about the impact artificial intelligence will have on software companies have prompted more Wall Street analysts to recommend selling Adobe Inc. and Salesforce Inc. stock than they have in ye

items.title: SpaceX (SPCX) Set to Unlock $116B in Shares After IPO Restrictions Lift
items.description: SpaceX has set the stage for one of the largest share unlocks in capital markets history, with as much as $116 billion worth of stock becoming eligible for sale for the first time next month.

items.title: US Yields Hit Two-Month High as Oil Sparks Inflation Risk
items.description: US Treasuries fell, pushing 10- and 30-year yields to the highest in about two months, as a surge in oil prices stoked concern that inflationary pressures will prompt the Federal Reserve to raise inte

items.title: BlackRock vs BlackRock: Aston Martin Debt Revamp Risks In-House Clash
items.description: A potential debt revamp at Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings Plc is setting the stage for an unusual battle between different divisions of asset management giant BlackRock Inc.

items.title: Colombia, Peru Leaders Draw Investors With Policy Signals
items.description: Investors are finding fresh reasons to buy Colombian and Peruvian assets as the countries’ newly elected presidents deliver early signals that their market-friendly agendas will translate into policy

items.title: Nasdaq 100 Notches Best Day in Weeks as Chipmakers Rebound
items.description: The Nasdaq 100 Index posted its best day in three weeks on Tuesday as a rebound in chipmakers at the center of the artificial-intelligence trade overshadowed the re-escalation in the US-Iran war.

items.title: High Yielders Pace Emerging-Market Currency Gains, Stocks Jump
items.description: Emerging-market currencies were mixed on Tuesday, with high-yielding names outperforming as investors looked past the latest developments in the Middle East and focused on carry trades instead.

items.title: UK Yields Stall Near Two-Month High in Warning for Burnham
items.description: There’s been no honeymoon in the bond market for the UK’s new Prime Minister Andy Burnham.

items.title: Gold and Silver Rise on Dip-Buying as Traders Weigh Fed Strategy
items.description: Gold and silver advanced on dip-buying as traders monitored Middle East tensions for signs that higher energy costs could stoke inflation, putting pressure on the Federal Reserve to tighten policy.

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 21
items.description: Brent oil advanced to the highest since early June as traders weighed threats to maritime traffic that span from the Strait of Hormuz and the Red Sea to a key Kazakh export terminal on Russia’s coast.

items.title: Stocks Climb as Rebound in Chipmakers Gathers Pace: Markets Wrap
items.description: Wall Street staged a comeback as dip buyers emerged, boosting stocks amid a rally in chipmakers on bets the artificial-intelligence trade that’s powered the bull market has more room to run.
